title: Mullin Masters the Wind at Deal
title_candidates: Mullin Masters the Wind at Deal
Deal Me In: Mullin Takes Both Leads
Sand, Wind and a Ten: Round One at Royal Cinque Ports
theme: David Mullin's mid-round purple patch hands him both leads, while Alex Baker's links nightmare leaves him chasing on Stableford and adrift on Gross
opening_hook: Open on a windswept Deal morning — the championship links is supposed to bite, and within an hour it has, with Gregg Williams already 4-over on a single hole
foreshadow: Gregg Williams's quadruple at the 3rd setting an early tone for what the course was going to extract
Alex Baker bleeding six shots across holes 3-5, a slow puncture that prefigured the bigger numbers to come at the 11th and 16th
Jon Baker slipping to the foot of the Spoon race as early as the 4th — a position he never escaped

competition_state.name: Trophy
competition_state.note: Mullin drew level on Stableford at the 11th in the middle of his hot run, then took the outright lead at the 16th and never let it go. Holds a three-point cushion over Alex Baker heading into R2.

competition_state.name: Green Jacket
competition_state.note: A gross-score race that ebbed and flowed early — Alex Baker level at the 6th, Mullin level by the 9th — before Baker's back-nine carnage settled it. Mullin ends the round five gross shots clear of Jon Baker.

competition_state.name: Wooden Spoon
competition_state.note: Jon Baker dropped to the bottom of the Spoon pile as early as the 4th and stayed there, four points adrift of Gregg Williams after eighteen.

players.arc: Quietly built the round of the day around a 13-point burst from the 9th to 12th, took both leads in the middle of the round and refused to give them back despite a wobble at the 13th.
Started the bleeding early (six shots gone across holes 3-5), then hammered the nails in himself with an 8 at the 11th and a 10 at the 16th — second on Stableford but well off the Gross pace.
Set the tone for the day's misery with a 9 at the 3rd and never quite recovered, finishing a distant third on Stableford.
Slid to the foot of the Spoon race at the 4th and despite a tidy par-par-par run from 11-13, couldn't claw his way out by the end of the round.

venue_notes: Royal Cinque Ports — TEG's 2nd visit to this championship Kent links, a former Open venue that punishes loose play when the wind blows. The scorecard suggests the wind blew.
